name: EPIDURAL HEMATOMA OF BRAIN
definition: EXTRADURAL HEMATOMA hematoma within potential space between naked inner table of skull + calvarial periosteum

demographics.Incidence: 2% of all serious head injuries; in < 1% of all children with cranial trauma;
demographics.Age: more common in younger patients 20-40 years ← dura more easily stripped away from

dominant_finding: fracture line in area of epidural hematoma
discriminating_features: fracture line in area of epidural hematoma
expanding biconvex (lenticular = elliptical) extra-axial fluid collection (most frequent) =
usually does not cross suture lines
separation of venous sinuses / falx from inner table

CT: fracture line in area of epidural hematoma
expanding biconvex (lenticular = elliptical) extra-axial fluid collection (most frequent) =
usually does not cross suture lines
separation of venous sinuses / falx from inner table
fresh extravasated blood (30-50 HU) / coagulated blood (50-80 HU) in acute stage
rarely with hypoattenuated "swirl" ← admixture of fresh blood into clotted blood during
mass effect ("compression cone effect") with effacement of gyri + sulci from:
signs of arterial injury (rare): contrast extravasation, arteriovenous fistula, middle
MR: low intensity of fibrous dura mater allows differentiation of epidural from subdural blood
ANGIO: meningeal arteries displaced away from inner table of skull

clinical.Rx: after surgical evacuation return of ventricular system to midline
